recentResults.highlight: Sooryavanshi 103(37) — 2nd-fastest IPL ton ever, 12 sixes; Ishan Kishan 74(31) PoM & Abhishek 57(29) put on 132 in 55 balls; Cummins returns as captain, takes 2/30 — SRH 4th win in a row
Kagiso Rabada 3/25 PoM rips through CSK powerplay; Sai Sudharsan 87(46), 7 sixes — GT's FIRST EVER win at Chepauk; Gaikwad 74* lone hand, CSK drop to 6th

recentResults.result: Match tied (KKR won the Super Over)
recentResults.venue: Bharat Ratna Shri Atal Bihari Vajpayee Ekana Cricket Stadium, Lucknow
recentResults.highlight: Rinku Singh 83*(51) PoM, 4 sixes off Rathi in 20th — KKR rescued from 93/7 (Mohsin 5/23 maiden T20 5-fer); Shami last-ball 6 ties it; Narine super over: Pooran bowled, Markram caught — LSG bottom of table
Hazlewood 4/12 PoM & Bhuvi 3/5 (DC 8/6 in 4 overs) — DC's lowest of season, lowest match aggregate (152) ever for DC vs RCB; Kohli 23*, hits 9000 IPL runs & 300 sixes; RCB up to 2nd at 12 pts

recentResults.venue: Maharaja Yadavindra Singh International Cricket Stadium, Mullanpur
recentResults.highlight: RR END PBKS UNBEATEN RUN — Stoinis 62*(22), Prabhsimran 59 lift PBKS to 222; Sooryavanshi 27(11) blitz, Jaiswal 51(27), Ferreira 50*(26) & Dubey finish 228-chase; Chahal 3-fer in vain — RR jump to 3rd, PBKS taste first defeat at home

storylines.title: PBKS' unbeaten run ends — RR chase 223 in 19.2 overs at Mullanpur
storylines.body: Punjab Kings tasted their first defeat of IPL 2026 on April 28 as Rajasthan Royals chased down 223 with four balls to spare at Mullanpur, finishing 228/4. PBKS had posted what looked like a winning 222/4 thanks to Marcus Stoinis's stunning 62* off 22 (24 runs off the last over of Brijesh Sharma) and Prabhsimran Singh's 59 off 44. But RR opted to bowl, and Vaibhav Sooryavanshi's 27 off 11 in the powerplay (84/1 first six overs) plus Yashasvi Jaiswal's 26-ball 51 set the platform. Yuzvendra Chahal turned the match briefly with 3 wickets for 16 (Jaiswal, Jurel, Parag), reducing RR to 151/4. But Donovan Ferreira (50* off 26) and impact sub Shubham Dubey iced it from there. Sooryavanshi's powerplay knock takes him past 400 runs — he's now Orange Cap leader at SR 238. PBKS now 6-1-1, still top with 13 points but no longer unbeaten. RR jump to 3rd with 12 points.

storylines.title: 15-year-old Sooryavanshi takes Orange Cap — 400 runs at SR 238, 37 sixes already
storylines.body: Vaibhav Sooryavanshi has stormed to the top of the Orange Cap. After his 27 off 11 in RR's record chase against PBKS, the teenager now has 400 runs in 9 innings — at a strike rate of 238.10, the highest by anyone in the top 30 of the run-scorer list. His 37 sixes lead the entire tournament, ahead of even Abhishek Sharma (28). For perspective: Sooryavanshi has scored more sixes in 9 innings than Klaasen, Kohli, KL Rahul or Gill have in 8. He averages 44 even with two ducks. The kid's powerplay numbers are absurd — he's averaging close to 30 runs off 13 balls in the first 6 overs. With CSK and PBKS having fallen prey to his explosive starts, every team's bowling plan now has 'how do we contain Sooryavanshi in the powerplay' as the first item. The fact that he's leading IPL 2026's batting charts at 15 years old is one of the most extraordinary stories in cricket history.

storylines.title: DC bowled out for 75 — Hazlewood 4/12, Bhuvi 3/5 expose Delhi's powerplay nightmare
storylines.body: Delhi Capitals were demolished for just 75 — the lowest total of IPL 2026 and the smallest aggregate (152) ever in a DC vs RCB match. Just 48 hours after scoring 264, DC found themselves 8 for 6 inside 4 overs as Bhuvneshwar Kumar (3/5) and Josh Hazlewood (4/12 PoM) shared seven wickets. KL Rahul, Nitish Rana and Tristan Stubbs all gone in the powerplay. Abishek Porel's 30 was the highest score; Miller (19) and Jamieson (12) gave DC a fig leaf. RCB chased 76 in 6.3 overs — Padikkal 34*, Bethell 20, Kohli a brutal 23* including the six that brought up his 9000 IPL runs (first ever) and 300th career IPL six. Captain Rajat Patidar admitted even he was surprised; Axar Patel said "I still cannot understand what happened." RCB jump to 2nd with 12 points; DC slip to 7th and have now lost 3 in a row.

storylines.title: Kohli's twin milestones — first to 9000 IPL runs and 300 IPL sixes, both in the same chase
storylines.body: Virat Kohli used the smallest of stages — a 76-run chase against DC — to hit two career-defining marks within the same six-ball over. He clipped Natarajan to long-off to bring up 9000 IPL runs (the first batter ever to reach the figure), then followed up with two flat sixes — one back over the bowler's head, one over deep midwicket — that were also his 299th and 300th sixes in IPL history. Kohli has now scored 351 runs in 8 matches at SR 162.50, jumping to 4th in the Orange Cap race. He's also leapfrogged MS Dhoni in IPL all-time runs. With RCB now up to 2nd at 12 points and unbeaten at home this season, Kohli's farewell-watch chatter has only intensified.

storylines.title: Bhuvneshwar's 7-runs-an-over genius takes Purple Cap — RCB pacer joins Kamboj and Malinga on 14
storylines.body: Bhuvneshwar Kumar's 3/5 against DC moved him to 14 wickets in 8 matches — joint leader of the Purple Cap with Anshul Kamboj and Eshan Malinga, but with the lowest economy of all leading wicket-takers at just 7.61. He's now bowled 31 overs in IPL 2026 and conceded 236 runs total. Three Indians at the top of the chart (Bhuvi, Kamboj, Malinga – the latter is from SRH); Jofra Archer and Prince Yadav round out the top 5 on 13 each. The bowling race is now a five-team scramble: every match swings the lead. Notable: Bhuvi has not picked up a 4-fer this season — just relentless 2 and 3-wicket spells.

storylines.title: Gujarat's Chepauk hoodoo ends — Rabada 3/25 and Sai Sudharsan 87 break a curse
storylines.body: Gujarat Titans had never won at MA Chidambaram Stadium. They ticked that box on April 26 in emphatic fashion — beating CSK by 8 wickets. Kagiso Rabada (3/25 PoM) and Mohammed Siraj's powerplay burst left CSK at 24/3 before they could blink, with the pace and bounce no one expected on a dry pitch. Ruturaj Gaikwad fought hard with 74* off 60 (his first IPL fifty in 11 innings), but no other CSK batter passed 18. Sai Sudharsan, fresh off his hundred vs RCB, delivered 87 off 46 with 7 sixes — fastest to 2000 IPL runs (47 innings). GT brought up their hundred quicker than CSK brought up their fifty. GT climb to 5th, CSK drop to 6th and now sit at 3-5 with their playoff hopes hanging by a thread.

auctionHighlights.note: Most expensive overseas buy in IPL history — KKR rebuild after Super Over win; Green still finding his feet
Sri Lanka slinger — calf/hamstring injury from T20 World Cup has kept him sidelined most of the season
Joint record uncapped buy — UP all-rounder picked from base Rs.30 lakh after 5-team bidding war
Joint record uncapped buy — 19yo Rajasthan wicket-keeper batter, serial six-hitter (28 sixes in 12 T20s)
England all-rounder — SRH on a 4-match winning streak, sit 3rd at 10 points after Cummins' return
Australia wicket-keeper — LSG hit rock bottom (10th, 6 losses in a row), the season's biggest collapse
J&K pacer — DC's impact-sub regular as Capitals slump to 7th with 3 straight losses
Leg-spinner — RR slip to 4th at 5-3, still a key wicket-taker as Royals fight for top 4
Australian all-rounder — PBKS still top at 13 points (6-1-1) after first loss to RR ended unbeaten run
South Africa opener — 112*(60) vs PBKS remains his only big knock; MI down to 9th at 2-5
Left-arm pacer — 4/24 vs GT matched his IPL-best, a quiet bargain buy in MI's bowling attack
West Indies all-rounder (back after 2 years) — GT's first-ever Chepauk win lifts them to 5th; took the Kohli wicket at Chinnaswamy
